{{Quote|In a monthlong campaign, the New Republic pushed Pellaeon back, but suffered a grave defeat in the Battle of Orinda.|[[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al]]|<ref name="TNEC-" />}}
The '''Orinda campaign''' was waged by the newly-formed [[Imperial Remnant]] against the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] in [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]] during the low-intensity phase of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. This was to be the very first campaign by [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] in his capacity as Supreme Commander and was able to repel the Republic offensive, prolonging the conflict.

==Prelude==
{{Main|Imperial Reunification}}
Following the failed offensive against the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] in early [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]] and the subsequent retirement of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Natasi Daala|Daala]], [[Vice Admiral/Legends|Vice Admiral]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] assumed command of Imperial forces in the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]]. Before abandoning the Deep Core and retreating to the [[Pentastar Alignment]], Pellaeon salvaged cached equipment located by analyzing the records he had inherited. Among the bounty were the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyers]] [[Dominion (Star Dreadnought)|''Dominion'']] and ''[[Megador]]'', both of which had been abandoned intact at the [[Deep 3]] shipyard.<ref name="TEGtW" /> When Pellaeon absorbed the [[Pentastar Alignment]], these two warships were joined by the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class]] [[Reaper (Executor-class)|''Reaper'']].<ref name="EssChrono">[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']]</ref> ''Reaper'' would serve as the Admiral's flagship in the coming campaign.

The three [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyers]] gave Pellaeon enormous leverage, and he was made Supreme Commander of the Remnant's forces by the [[Council of Moffs/Legends|Council of Moffs]]. With his new authority, Pellaeon resolved to wage a decisive offensive to restore Imperial morale, and demonstrate to the New Republic that the now [[Imperial Remnant]] was still a power to be reckoned with.<ref name="TEGtW" />

==The campaign==
===Preparations===
[[Orinda/Legends|Orinda]] was chosen as the primary target. Symbolically, the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] trade world held value as one of the [[New Order/Legends|New Order's]] greatest successes outside the Core, and was also the former seat of the [[Imperial Ruling Council/Legends|Imperial Ruling Council]]; Pellaeon also felt that the demonstration of strength displayed in retaking it would give the New Republic pause without provoking an overwhelming response.<ref name="TEGtW" />

The campaign would rely upon the New Republic remaining ignorant of the existence of [[Dominion (Star Dreadnought)|''Dominion'']] and ''[[Megador]]'' until the crucial moment. ''[[Megador]]'' was deployed to [[Agamar/Legends|Agamar]] to guard the Empire's eastern flank, while ''Reaper'' embarked the elite [[181st Imperial Fighter Wing/Legends|181st Imperial Fighter Group]] under [[General/Legends|General]] [[Turr Phennir]] at [[Borosk/Legends|Borosk]].<ref name="TEGtW" />

===Orinda captured===
{{Quote|Six months after Daala relinquished her command, Pellaeon made an aggressive lunge at the New Republic by seizing the small planet Orinda. By the time General Antilles mounted a counterattack, Pellaeon had already captured six neighboring systems.|Voren Na'al|<ref name="TNEC-" />}}
{{Main|Raid on Orinda|Battle of Lonnaw}}
Pellaeon and Phennir struck Coreward along the [[Entralla Route/Legends|Entralla Route]], [[Raid on Orinda|capturing Orinda]] and six other systems within a few weeks before [[Battle of Lonnaw|digging in]] at [[Lonnaw]].<ref name="TEGtW" /> At the same time, there was an attempt to covertly subjugate [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]] and its [[Witches of Dathomir|witches]].

Once entrenched at Lonnaw, Pellaeon sent terms to [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]]: the Republic was told that Orinda had been reclaimed to secure the Imperial Remnant's borders and for its cultural significance it. Pellaeon sincerely added that if the New Republic respected the claim, the Imperial Remnant would refrain from further offensive action.

===New Republic response and politics===
The New Republic was unconvinced by Pellaeon's pledge to respect the new border, and were panicked by the reappearance of the [[Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnoughts]] as tools of war. It saw the capture of Orinda as the first phase of a renewed Imperial push to the Core. The counter-offensive was spearheaded by the ''Executor''-class ''[[Lusankya/Legends|Lusankya]]'', commanded by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], and [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]].<ref name="TEGtW" />

The deployment of ''Lusankya'' was controversial within the New Republic military. The fleet was moving toward smaller warships and starfighters, as embodied by the [[Defender (ship-design program)|Defender]] and [[New Class Modernization Program]]s; ''Lusankya'' was seen by many as an anachronism, a relic of the Galactic Empire's emphasis on large capital ships. But events during the campaign would cause a partial shift in the thinking of the New Republic military and helped affirm the place of larger capital ships in its fleet. Until then, however, military politics would hamper the New Republic's performance in the campaign.<ref name="TEGtW" />

===Imperial withdrawal===
''Reaper'' and ''Lusankya'', as well as the 181st and [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], fought numerous skirmishes in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. Pellaeon himself [[Battle of Corthenia|took Corthenia]] aboard the ''Reaper'', while Phennir's 181st [[Battle of Hewett|conquered Hewett]] and [[Battle of Tartaglia|Tartaglia]]. The ''Lusankya'' and the ''Reaper'' [[Battle of Darkon|clashed at Darkon]], and [[Battle of the Traval-Pacor system|again at Traval-Pacor]]. An [[Battle of Orocco|indecisive battle]] was fought between the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] groups at [[Orocco]], and Antilles' drive up the Entralla Route was [[Battle of Tyan|halted]] by the 181st at [[Tyan]].

While the public on both sides were enthralled by the duel, the indecisive nature of these encounters induced the New Republic to deploy [[Endurance (Endurance-class)|''Endurance'']], the lead ship of [[Endurance-class fleet carrier|her class of fleet carrier]], and her complement of [[E-7 E-wing multi-role starfighter|E-wings]]. ''Endurance'' was commanded by Admiral [[Areta Bell]], who was under great pressure to prove the superiority of starfighters over capital ships, and that of the E-wing in particular.<ref name="TEGtW" />

With both the ''Lusankya'' and the ''Endurance'' advancing, Pellaeon ordered a gradual withdrawal toward Orinda and [[Battle of Obredaan|conceded Obredaan]]. The New Republic fleet obliged by pursuing ''Reaper'', which they thought to be the last Super Star Destroyer.<ref name="TEGtW" />

[[File:LusankyavsReaper-EGW.jpg||thumb|right|250px|The ''Lusankya'' and the ''Reaper'' engage at Orinda.]]

===Disaster at Orinda===
{{Quote|There, the ''Reaper'' destroyed most of Antilles's starfighters by annihilating the ﬂeet carrier ''Endurance''. Rogue Squadron, stationed aboard the ''Lusankya'', covered the ﬂeet's retreat. The New Republic chose to leave Orinda in imperial hands, and instead fortified the surrounding systems.|Voren Na'al|<ref name="TNEC-" />}}
{{Main|Battle of Orinda}}
The Imperial and Republic fleets engaged at Orinda, with ''Reaper'' and ''Lusankya'' squaring off against one another and exchanging broadsides. However, Bell regarded the defenses of the ''Reaper'' to be far too strong for her E-wings, and delayed launching them until its defenses had been reduced further. Once both sides were entangled, Pellaeon sprung his trap: [[Dominion (Star Dreadnought)|''Dominion'']] and six [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Immobilizer 418 cruisers]] arrived to trap the New Republic fleet in [[Gravity well/Legends|gravity wells]]. ''Endurance'' was destroyed with her fighters still in their hangars, and ''Lusankya'' only barely managed to escape after Rogue Squadron broke through the Interdictor screen.<ref name="TEGtW" />

The New Republic expedition ended as a debacle. Orinda was abandoned in favor of reinforcing nearby systems.

===Dathomir===
The subjugation of Dathomir was foiled by an alliance of convenience between several famous [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] soldiers and the renegade [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Royal Guardsman]] [[Kir Kanos]].

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|Despite few scattered victories, including the Battle of Orinda and the requisition of his former flagship Chimaera in the Battle of Gravlex Med, he had been unable to stop the march of history.|Voren Na'al, about Admiral Pellaeon|<ref name="TNEC-" />}}
The campaign was a resounding success for Empire. However, that same success only inspired the [[Moff/Legends|Moffs]] to order an ill-conceived offensive into the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Borderland Regions]] [[13 ABY/Legends|the next year]]. The ''Reaper'' was subsequently lost at the [[Battle of Celanon]].<ref name="TEGtW" /><ref name="EssChrono" /> For the most part, the Orinda campaign was followed by five years of relative calm in the Civil War, as the New Republic was largely occupied with consolidation and dealing with internal crises with [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] and the [[Duskhan League]]. The calm would be broken by a major Imperial [[Imperial Skirmishes|offensive]] in [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]], followed by [[Pellaeon–Gavrisom Treaty|peace accords]] [[19 ABY/Legends|two years later]].

The performance of the ''Reaper'' in the Orinda campaign was one of a number of factors along with [[Operation Shadow Hand]] and the [[Black Fleet Crisis]] that encouraged the New Republic to begin the development of its own dreadnought analogues, namely the [[Mediator-class battle cruiser|''Mediator''-class battle cruiser]] and the [[Viscount-class Star Defender|''Viscount''-class Star Defender]].
